steps.code:
<?php
require_once 'wordpress/wp-load.php';
$dir = WP_CONTENT_DIR . '/mu-plugins';
if ( ! is_dir( $dir ) ) {
	wp_mkdir_p( $dir );
}
$file = $dir . '/blockparty-modal-playground-svg-kses.php';
$lines = array(
	'<?php',
	'add_filter( \'wp_kses_allowed_html\', function( $tags, $context ) {',
	'	if ( \'post\' !== $context ) {',
	'		return $tags;',
	'	}',
	'	$tags[\'dialog\'] = array(',
	'		\'aria-modal\' => true,',
	'		\'class\' => true,',
	'		\'style\' => true,',
	'		\'id\' => true,',
	'		\'closedby\' => true,',
	'	);',
	'	$tags[\'svg\'] = array(',
	'		\'xmlns\' => true,',
	'		\'viewbox\' => true,',
	'		\'width\' => true,',
	'		\'height\' => true,',
	'		\'fill\' => true,',
	'		\'class\' => true,',
	'		\'aria-hidden\' => true,',
	'		\'role\' => true,',
	'		\'focusable\' => true,',
	'	);',
	'	$tags[\'path\'] = array(',
	'		\'d\' => true,',
	'		\'fill\' => true,',
	'		\'class\' => true,',
	'	);',
	'	return $tags;',
	'}, 10, 2 );',
);
file_put_contents( $file, implode( "\n", $lines ) . "\n" );
echo file_exists( $file ) ? 'mu-plugin installed' : 'mu-plugin failed';
?>

Open my modal window

My Modal

Blockparty Modal is a WordPress plugin that lets you add accessible modal dialogs to your content via the Gutenberg block editor. You define the modal content and behaviour in the editor; on the frontend, the modal is shown when the user activates a linked trigger (such as a button block).
